questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Debentures are long-term borrowings of a company.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Issue of debentures involves creation of a new debt by a company.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The issue of debentures indeed involves creation of a new debt by a company, which is a long-term borrowing.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Redemptive debentures can be redeemed at any time.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Redemptive debentures are issued at a premium and can be redeemed at par.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Redemptive debentures can be redeemed at any time, not necessarily at par.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Debenture trust deeds can be modified by a simple majority vote.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Debenture trust deeds are registered deeds and cannot be modified without the consent of the debenture-holders.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Debenture trust deeds are registered deeds and cannot be modified without the consent of the debenture-holders, not by a simple majority vote.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Debenture-holders have a priority claim over the assets of the company.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Debenture-holders are secured creditors of the company.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is true as debenture-holders have a priority claim over the assets of the company, as they are secured creditors.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Debentures can be issued at a discount.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Debentures can be issued at a premium only.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is true as debentures can be issued at a discount, not just a premium.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The debenture trust deed is a document which shows the terms and conditions of debentures.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The debenture trust deed is a document which shows the terms and conditions of the debenture-holders.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is true as the debenture trust deed indeed shows the terms and conditions of debentures, which also include the terms and conditions of the debenture-holders.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Debentures are a type of share capital.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Debentures are a type of long-term loan capital.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is false as debentures are a type of long-term loan capital, not share capital.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Unsecured debentures are issued at a premium.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Unsecured debentures can be issued at a discount or at a premium.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A

(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The issue of debentures involves creation of a new share capital.

opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A

(C) A is true but R is false
